prompts.title: EVENT_LOG_AND_REPLAY_DOCTRINE-0
prompts.purpose: Freeze constitutional event, replay, and operational-log doctrine without promoting convenience logs into semantic truth.
stop_conditions: event or replay law collapses truth/perceived/render separation
event or replay law bypasses ownership, bridge, or lifecycle doctrine

prompts.title: SNAPSHOT_SERVICE_MODEL-0
prompts.purpose: Freeze lawful snapshot-service doctrine that consumes state externalization and lifecycle law rather than replacing them.
stop_conditions: snapshot law traps authoritative truth in hidden runtime state
snapshot law blurs bridge-sensitive or ownership-sensitive state boundaries

prompts.title: BUILD_GRAPH_LOCK-0
prompts.purpose: Consolidate existing build-graph substrate into explicit constitutional lock doctrine.
stop_conditions: build lock would silently rewrite release authority or replace existing substrate

prompts.title: PRESET_CONSOLIDATION-0
prompts.purpose: Converge existing preset and toolchain surfaces without inferring canon from convenience.
stop_conditions: preset consolidation would flatten platform distinctions or invent a second build canon

prompts.title: VERSIONING_CONSTITUTION-0
prompts.purpose: Freeze explicit version meaning, identity, and compatibility doctrine across release surfaces.
stop_conditions: version doctrine cannot align compatibility and identity without silent reinterpretation

prompts.title: RELEASE_INDEX_AND_RESOLUTION_POLICY-0
prompts.purpose: Align current release index and resolution surfaces into one constitutional model.
stop_conditions: index or resolution policy would smuggle in trust or publication authority

prompts.title: RELEASE_CONTRACT_PROFILE-0
prompts.purpose: Freeze an explicit release contract profile grounded in current release identity and update surfaces.
stop_conditions: release contract meaning conflicts with canon, compatibility law, or ownership cautions

prompts.title: ARTIFACT_AND_TARGET_NAMING_POLICY-0
prompts.purpose: Tighten artifact and target naming around release contract and version law.
stop_conditions: naming would silently become release authority rather than derived policy

prompts.title: CHANGELOG_POLICY-0
prompts.purpose: Define lawful change-record expectations across current changelog surfaces.
stop_conditions: change-record law drifts into compatibility or release identity reinterpretation

prompts.title: RELEASE_PIPELINE_AND_ARCHIVE_MODEL-0
prompts.purpose: Formalize existing archive, manifest, and pipeline substrate without granting autonomous publication authority.
stop_conditions: pipeline or archive law would grant publication or trust authority by convenience

prompts.title: OPERATOR_TRANSACTION_LOG_MODEL-0
prompts.purpose: Freeze constitutional operator transaction-log doctrine tied to replay, snapshot, and release resolution continuity.
stop_conditions: transaction continuity cannot be reconciled with replay, snapshot, or release resolution law

prompts.title: DISASTER_DOWNGRADE_AND_YANK_POLICY-0
prompts.purpose: Freeze downgrade and yank policy without normalizing live publication authority.
stop_conditions: downgrade or yank law assumes trust or live-ops powers not yet formalized

prompts.title: SANDBOXING_AND_ISOLATION_MODEL-0
prompts.purpose: Freeze isolation doctrine for tool, runtime, and operational surfaces without overstating guarantees.
stop_conditions: sandboxing claims exceed current safety, ownership, or runtime-boundary law

prompts.title: MULTI_VERSION_COEXISTENCE_REVIEW-0
prompts.purpose: Review whether lawful multi-version coexistence is supportable after core Υ foundations land.
stop_conditions: coexistence would normalize ambiguous package, schema, or runtime ownership
coexistence requires hidden compatibility reinterpretation

prompts.title: PUBLICATION_TRUST_AND_LICENSING_REVIEW-0
prompts.purpose: Review publication, trust, and licensing doctrine without operationalizing casual automation.
stop_conditions: review drifts into direct publication automation or trust-root mutation

prompts.title: HOTSWAP_BOUNDARY_REVIEW-0
prompts.purpose: Review whether hotswap boundaries can be defined without violating lifecycle, state, and authority continuity.
blocking_conditions: transaction or downgrade law remains incomplete
stop_conditions: hotswap would redefine lifecycle continuity or operator authority by convenience

prompts.title: DISTRIBUTED_AUTHORITY_FOUNDATIONS_REVIEW-0
prompts.purpose: Review whether stronger distributed-authority law is supportable after continuity, downgrade, trust, and coexistence prerequisites land.
blocking_conditions: trust and publication review remains incomplete
stop_conditions: distributed authority would require live-ops assumptions or ownership transfer semantics not yet frozen
